content.problemStatement:
**This is the hard version of the problem. Here, P2iP_{2i}P2i‚Äč may or may not be zero.**

You are given a permutation‚Ä†^\dagger‚Ä† PPP of the integers 111 to 2N2N2N, with some of its elements missing (represented by 000's).

Find the number of ways of filling in the zeros to obtain a valid permutation, such that the quantity

‚ą£P1‚ąíP2‚ą£+‚ą£P3‚ąíP4‚ą£+‚Ä¶+‚ą£P2N‚ąí1‚ąíP2N‚ą£|P_1 - P_2| + |P_3 - P_4| + \ldots + |P_{2N-1} - P_{2N}|‚ą£P1‚Äč‚ąíP2‚Äč‚ą£+‚ą£P3‚Äč‚ąíP4‚Äč‚ą£+‚Ä¶+‚ą£P2N‚ąí1‚Äč‚ąíP2N‚Äč‚ą£is maximized.

The answer can be large, so find it modulo 998244353998 244 353998244353.

‚Ä†^\dagger‚Ä† A permutation of the integers 111 to NNN is an array of length NNN that contains every integer from 111 to NNN exactly once.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN ‚ÄĒ meaning that PPP has length 2N2N2N.
The second line contains 2N2N2N space-separated integers P1,P2,‚Ä¶,P2NP_1, P_2, \ldots, P_{2N}P1‚Äč,P2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,P2N‚Äč.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of ways of filling in the zeros to maximize the sum, modulo 998244353998 244 353998244353.
constraints: All the non-zero elements of PPP are distinct.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚čÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.
